task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly calculates the median of a given list of numbers, handling both odd and even lengths and returning None for an empty input.
output:
```python
def median(nums):
    if not nums:
        return None

    sorted_nums = sorted(nums)

    middle_index = len(sorted_nums) // 2
    middle_value = (sorted_nums[middle_index - 1] + sorted_nums[middle_index]) / 2 if len(sorted_nums) % 2 == 0 else sorted_nums[middle_index]

    return middle_value
```
trace:
In this implementation, I first check if the input list is empty and return None as per the requirement. Then I sort the list and calculate the index of the middle element (rounding down for odd length lists and averaging two middle elements for even length ones). The last step is to return the calculated middle value as a float.

An edge case that was handled is when the list has no elements, in which case the function returns None instead of raising an error or returning an empty list.
